headline: Honda Recalls 2025-2026 CRF250R and CRF450R Off-Road Motorcycles Over Handlebar Clamp Safety Risk
description: American Honda recalls about 12,600 2025-2026 CRF250R and CRF450R off-road motorcycles due to handlebar clamp defect posing crash hazard risk.
keywords: Model Year 2025-2026 CRF250R/RX/RWE and CRF450R/RX/RWE Off-Road Motorcycles
The handlebar's clamp can unexpectedly cause the handlebar to rotate, posing a risk of serious injury or death due to a crash hazard.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: American Honda is recalling approximately 12,600 model year 2025-2026 CRF250R/RX/RWE and CRF450R/RX/RWE off-road motorcycles because the handlebar clamp can unexpectedly rotate, increasing the risk of a crash. Consumers should stop using the motorcycles immediately and contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer for a free inspection and repair or replacement of the clamp.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves model year 2025-2026 Honda off-road motorcycles with model numbers CRF250R, CRF250RX, CRF250RWE, CRF450R, CRF450RX, and CRF450RWE. These motorcycles feature a red chassis and have the white Honda logo printed on both sides of the rear. The model number is displayed in white letters on the front of the bike. The vehicle identification number (VIN) is located on the left side of the steering head.

About 12,600 units were sold nationwide through authorized Honda Powersports dealers between June 2024 and March 2026, with an additional six units sold in Canada. The motorcycles retailed for prices ranging from $8,900 to $13,200. The recall was announced on April 30, 2026.

The recall was initiated after the firm received one report of a crash related to the handlebar clamp defect. No injuries have been reported to date.

products.Name: Model Year 2025-2026 CRF250R/RX/RWE and CRF450R/RX/RWE Off-Road Motorcycles
products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,600 (In addition, about six were sold in Canada)

hazards: The handlebar's clamp can unexpectedly cause the handlebar to rotate, posing a risk of serious injury or death due to a crash hazard.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled off-road motorcycles immediately and contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer to schedule an appointment for a free inspection and if necessary, a replacement of the handlebar's clamp at the dealer's location.
injuries: The firm has received one report of a crash. No injuries have been reported.
retailers: Authorized Honda Powersports dealers nationwide from June 2024 through March 2026 for between $8,900 and $13,200.
importers: American Honda Motor Co. Inc, of Torrance, California

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To determine if your motorcycle is included in this recall, check the following:

  • Model year: 2025 or 2026
  • Model number: CRF250R, CRF250RX, CRF250RWE, CRF450R, CRF450RX, or CRF450RWE
  • Model number location: printed in white letters on the front of the bike
  • Appearance: red chassis with a white Honda logo on both sides of the rear
  • VIN location: on the left side of the steering head

Consumers can verify if their VIN is included in the recall by visiting Honda’s recall website at https://powersports.honda.com/recalls.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you own one of the recalled motorcycles, stop using it immediately to avoid the risk of a crash. Contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer to schedule a free inspection of the handlebar clamp. If the clamp is found to be defective, the dealer will repair or replace it at no cost to you.

For more information or assistance, contact American Honda Motor toll-free at 866-784-1870, Monday through Friday from 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT, or visit https://powersports.honda.com/recalls.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The handlebar clamp on these recalled motorcycles can unexpectedly allow the handlebar to rotate during use. This unexpected rotation can cause the rider to lose control, increasing the risk of a crash. A crash resulting from this defect could lead to serious injury or death. The risk is significant enough that consumers are advised to stop using the motorcycles until the clamp is inspected and repaired or replaced.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects owners and riders of 2025-2026 Honda CRF250R, CRF250RX, CRF250RWE, CRF450R, CRF450RX, and CRF450RWE off-road motorcycles. These motorcycles were sold exclusively through authorized Honda Powersports dealers nationwide and a small number in Canada.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: American Honda Motor toll-free at 866-784-1870 from 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, or online at https://powersports.honda.com and click on "Recalls" at the top of the page for more information or at https://powersports.honda.com/recalls.

faq.answer: You can check your motorcycle’s model year, model number, and VIN against the recall details. The model number is on the front of the bike in white letters, and the VIN is located on the left side of the steering head. You can also verify your VIN at https://powersports.honda.com/recalls.

faq.question: What is the safety hazard with the recalled motorcycles?
faq.answer: The handlebar clamp can unexpectedly allow the handlebar to rotate, which may cause the rider to lose control and crash, posing a risk of serious injury or death.

faq.question: What should I do if my motorcycle is included in the recall?
faq.answer: Stop using the motorcycle immediately and contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer to schedule a free inspection and repair or replacement of the handlebar clamp.

headline: Build-A-Bear Workshop Recalls Heartwarming Hugs Bears Because of Zipper Choking Risk
description: Build-A-Bear recalls Heartwarming Hugs Bears due to detachable zipper slider posing choking hazard to children. About 36,000 units sold in US and Canada.
keywords: The zipper slider on the side pouch can detach, posing a risk of serious injury or death from choking hazard to children.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The Heartwarming Hugs weighted plush bears by Build-A-Bear Workshop are recalled because the zipper slider on the side pouch can detach, creating a choking hazard for children. Consumers should stop using these bears immediately and return them to Build-A-Bear for a refund or gift card.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves the Build-A-Bear Workshop Heartwarming Hugs weighted plush bear, model number 034464. The bear features a side pouch containing a heart-shaped bag filled with 2.5 pounds of ceramic beads that can be heated or cooled for comfort. The pouch is secured with a zipper on the side.

About 36,000 units were sold in the United States and an additional 520 units in Canada from January 2026 through March 2026 at Build-A-Bear Workshop stores and online at www.buildabear.com for approximately $48. The product is intended for children aged 3 years and older and includes a cautionary statement advising adult supervision due to the heated/cooled element.

There has been one report from the United Kingdom of the zipper slider detaching during use, but no injuries or incidents have been reported in the United States.

products.Name: Build-A-Bear Workshop Heartwarming Hugs Bears
products.NumberOfUnits: About 36,000 (In addition 520 were sold in Canada)

hazards: The zipper slider on the side pouch can detach, posing a risk of serious injury or death from choking hazard to children.
remedies: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Heartwarming Hugs Bear and return it to a local Build-A-Bear Workshop store to receive a refund in the form of original payment or a gift card for the original purchase price redeemable toward any available Make-Your-Own furry friend and any other available merchandise. If a consumer is unable to return their Heartwarming Hugs Bear to a local store, they can visit www.buildabear.com/recalls for more information or call Build-A-Bear to receive a free return shipping label to return the recalled bear and to receive a refund check in the form of original payment or in the form of a digital gift card to use on the firm's website.
injuries: There has been one consumer report in the United Kingdom of the zipper slider detaching while in use, and no reports in the United States. No injuries have been reported.
retailers: Build-A-Bear Workshop stores and online at www.buildabear.com from January 2026 through March 2026 for about $48.
importers: Build-A-Bear Workshop, Inc., of St Louis, Missouri

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify the recalled Heartwarming Hugs bear, look for the following details:

  • Model number 034464, located on the label sewn into the back side of one bear leg.
  • The bear has a side pouch with a zipper containing a heart-shaped bag filled with ceramic beads.
  • The bear is weighted with 2.5 pounds of ceramic beads inside the pouch, which can be heated or cooled.
  • The product is labeled for children aged 3 years and older and includes a cautionary statement about adult supervision.

Images of the recalled bear show the side pouch with the zipper and the heart-shaped bead bag inside.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you have a recalled Heartwarming Hugs bear, stop using it immediately. Consumers should return the bear to any local Build-A-Bear Workshop store to receive a refund. The refund can be issued as the original payment or as a gift card redeemable toward any Make-Your-Own furry friend or other merchandise available at Build-A-Bear.

If returning the bear to a store is not possible, visit www.buildabear.com/recalls or contact Build-A-Bear at 844-541-0144 (8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Saturday, 7 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT Sunday) or email ProductHotline@buildabear.com to request a free return shipping label. Upon return, consumers will receive a refund by original payment or a digital gift card for use on the Build-A-Bear website.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The zipper slider on the side pouch of the Heartwarming Hugs bear can detach unexpectedly. If a child puts the detached zipper slider in their mouth, it poses a serious choking hazard. This risk is particularly concerning for young children who may not recognize the danger of small parts. The hazard arises from the potential for the small zipper slider to block a child’s airway, which can lead to serious injury or death if not promptly addressed.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects anyone who owns a Build-A-Bear Workshop Heartwarming Hugs weighted plush bear with model number 034464 purchased between January and March 2026. The product is marketed for children aged 3 years and older. Parents, caregivers, and gift purchasers should check if they have this specific bear to prevent any risk to children.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Build-A-Bear at 844-541-0144 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Saturday and 7 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT on Sunday or by email at ProductHotline@buildabear.com, or online at https://www.buildabear.com/recalls or https://www.buildabear.com and click on "Recall" at the bottom of the page for more information.

faq.question: What is the specific hazard with the Heartwarming Hugs bear?
faq.answer: The zipper slider on the side pouch can detach, creating a choking hazard if a child puts the small part in their mouth.

faq.question: How can I tell if my Heartwarming Hugs bear is part of the recall?
faq.answer: Check the label sewn into the back side of one bear leg for model number 034464 and look for the side pouch with a zipper containing a heart-shaped bag filled with ceramic beads.

faq.question: What should I do if I have a recalled Heartwarming Hugs bear?
faq.answer: Stop using the bear immediately and return it to a Build-A-Bear Workshop store for a refund or contact Build-A-Bear for a free return shipping label and refund options.

headline: Recall Alert: Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends Toys Pose Serious Injury Risks
description: CBC Group recalls Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends children's toys due to choking and laceration hazards from a breakable plastic cube inside the plush toys.
keywords: Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends Children's Toys
The toys' plastic cube can break into small parts or sharp edges, posing a risk of serious injury from choking and laceration hazards.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends children's toys contain a removable plastic cube that can break into small, sharp pieces, posing choking and laceration hazards. Consumers should stop using the cube immediately and contact Stephan Baby for a refund or store credit.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves all Stephan Baby-branded Boo Bunnie and Friends children's toys. These toys are small plush animals featuring a removable, water-filled plastic cube designed to be chilled for comfort. The toys measure approximately 3.5 to 3.7 inches long, 2.5 to 3 inches tall, and 2.3 to 2.5 inches wide. They were sold in various colors and animal shapes.

The recalled toys were sold nationwide at independent stores and online through retailers including Stephanbaby.com, Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and Target.com from August 2017 through January 2026, priced between $9 and $2. About 227,500 units were sold in the U.S., with an additional 1,400 sold in Canada.

The recall was issued after 20 reports of the plastic cube cracking or breaking into small parts, including one incident where a child swallowed sharp pieces, highlighting the risk of serious injury from choking and lacerations.

Consumers with proof of purchase will receive a full refund. Consumers without proof of purchase will receive a $10 refund in the form of a check or a $20 stephanbaby.com store credit. Consumers will be asked to write in permanent marker "Recalled" on the indented area of the cube and upload a photo of the toy and the marked cube to https://recall.stephanbaby.com/ to receive a pre-paid shipping return label for the cube.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify the recalled toys, look for the following details:

  • The product is a small plush animal measuring about 3.5 to 3.7 inches long, 2.5 to 3 inches tall, and 2.3 to 2.5 inches wide.
  • The toy contains a removable, water-filled plastic cube designed to be chilled.
  • "Stephan BABY" is printed on a sewn-in label located along the side seam on the right underside of the plush toy.
  • "Stephan BABY" is also printed on the front and underside of the toy’s box packaging.

Refer to the product packaging or the sewn-in label to confirm if your toy is part of this recall.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you have a recalled Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends toy, immediately stop using the plastic cube and keep it away from children. Consumers should contact Stephan Baby to receive a refund or store credit.

Consumers with proof of purchase will receive a full refund. Those without proof of purchase can receive a $10 refund by check or a $20 store credit at stephanbaby.com.

To return the cube, write "Recalled" in permanent marker on the indented area of the cube, take a photo of the toy and the marked cube, and upload it to https://recall.stephanbaby.com/. You will receive a prepaid shipping label to send back the cube.

For more information or assistance, contact CBC Group toll-free at 877-725-5591 from 7 a.m. to 3 p.m. PT or email boobunnie@stephanbaby.com.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The removable plastic cube inside the Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends toys can break into small, sharp pieces. These fragments pose a choking hazard if swallowed by young children and can cause cuts or lacerations if handled. Because the cube is water-filled and designed to be chilled, it may be more prone to cracking or breaking during normal use.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects anyone who owns or cares for a Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends children's toy purchased between August 2017 and January 2026. The toys were sold nationwide and online, so families, caregivers, and gift buyers should check their products carefully.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: CBC Group, toll-free at 877-725-5591 from 7 a.m. to 3 p.m. PT, email at boobunnie@stephanbaby.com or online at https://recall.stephanbaby.com/ or https://stephanbaby.com/ and click on "Safety Recalls" at the bottom of the page for more information.

faq.question: What specific hazard does the Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie toy pose?
faq.answer: The toy's removable plastic cube can break into small, sharp pieces that pose choking and laceration hazards to children.

faq.question: How can I tell if my Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie toy is part of the recall?
faq.answer: Check for the "Stephan BABY" label sewn on the right underside side seam of the plush toy and on the front and underside of the packaging. The toy is a small plush animal with a removable, water-filled plastic cube.

faq.question: What should I do if I have a recalled toy but no proof of purchase?
faq.answer: You can still receive a $10 refund by check or a $20 store credit at stephanbaby.com by contacting Stephan Baby and following their return instructions.

headline: Recall of Travel Size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL Bottles for Lack of Child-Resistant Packaging
description: Bayer recalls 6 mL Afrin Original Nasal Spray bottles due to risk of child poisoning from non-child-resistant packaging. About 786,100 units sold at travel retailers.
keywords: Travel Size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL Bottles

The 6 mL nasal sprays contain an imidazoline, which must be in child-resistant packaging or meet the labeling requirements for non-complying packaging, as required by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. The 6 mL nasal spray's packaging is not child-resistant nor bears the required labeling statement, posing a risk of serious injury or illness from poisoning, if the contents are swallowed by young children.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Bayer is recalling about 786,100 travel size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL bottles because their packaging is not child-resistant as required, posing a poisoning risk to young children if swallowed. Consumers should secure these bottles immediately and request a refund through Bayer’s online form.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Travel Size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL bottles with specific lot numbers: 230361, 240822, 241198, 250066, 250152, 250646, and 250831. These bottles are labeled with "Afrin® Original Nasal Spray" and "1/5 FL OZ (6 mL)" on the front. No other Afrin sizes are included in this recall.

About 786,100 units were sold primarily at convenience stores and travel hubs, including airports, from September 2024 through April 2026, priced between $7 and $9. The product was manufactured by Bayer HealthCare LLC in Whippany, New Jersey, with manufacturing origins in Germany.

There have been no reported injuries related to this recall to date.

The 6 mL nasal spray's packaging is not child-resistant nor bears the required labeling statement, posing a risk of serious injury or illness from poisoning, if the contents are swallowed by young children.

Consumers will be asked to take and submit a photo of the product before disposing of it in order to receive a refund.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify recalled products, look for travel size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray bottles labeled "1/5 FL OZ (6 mL)" on the front. Check the lot number printed as a six-digit code followed by an expiration date in the format "YYYYMMM". The recalled lot numbers are 230361, 240822, 241198, 250066, 250152, 250646, and 250831. No other sizes or products are included in this recall.

Images of the front and back of the recalled bottles are available for comparison.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Consumers should immediately secure the recalled Afrin 6 mL bottles out of reach and sight of children. To receive a refund, visit the recall refund webform at www.livewell.bayer.com/afrin-original-spray-recall. You will be asked to submit a photo of the product before disposing of it. The webform can also be accessed via the "Safety Recall Info" button on the top right of www.afrin.com.

Do not use the product. Dispose of it only after completing the refund process.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled 6 mL Afrin nasal sprays contain an imidazoline ingredient that requires child-resistant packaging or specific labeling under the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. These bottles do not have child-resistant caps nor the required warning labels. This creates a risk that young children could accidentally swallow the contents, leading to serious injury or illness from poisoning.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects consumers who have purchased the travel size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL bottles with the specified lot numbers. These products were sold at convenience stores and travel locations such as airports between September 2024 and April 2026. Households with young children should be especially vigilant.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Bayer toll free at 800 317-2165, Monday through Friday 8 a.m. through 8 p.m. ET, or online through a recall refund webform available at www.livewell.bayer.com/afrin-original-spray-recall. The form can also be accessed by clicking the "Safety Recall Info" button located at the top right of the landing page www.afrin.com, which will take the consumer to the recall information page.

faq.answer: The bottles lack child-resistant packaging and required labeling, posing a risk of serious injury or illness if young children swallow the contents.

faq.answer: Consumers should visit www.livewell.bayer.com/afrin-original-spray-recall to submit a photo of the product and request a refund before disposing of the bottle.

headline: Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Button Cell Battery Ingestion
description: Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets sold on Amazon are recalled for accessible button cell batteries posing ingestion hazards to children. Consumers should stop use and seek.
keywords: The recalled bicycle lights violate the mandatory standard for consumer products with button cell and coin batteries because they contain button cell batteries that can be accessed easily by children, posing an ingestion hazard. Additionally, the bicycle lights do not have the warnings required under Reese's Law. When button cell or coin batteries are swallowed, the ingested batteries can cause serious injuries, internal chemical burns, and death.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets, sold on Amazon by Smfanlus, are recalled because their button cell batteries can be easily accessed by children, posing a serious ingestion hazard. Consumers should stop using these lights immediately and contact Smfanlus for a full refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets, models BL-01 and BL-02, which are encased in silicone and come in six colors: black, white, red, green, yellow, and blue. The lights are sold in packs of six (one of each color) and 24 (four of each color). Each light contains two button cell batteries.

Approximately 2,800 units were sold online at Amazon.com from March 2024 through February 2026, priced between $7 and $10 for the 6-pack and between $15 and $18 for the 24-pack. The product packaging features "QUMENEY" printed on labels at the top.

There have been no reported injuries related to this recall. The lights were distributed by Xiamen Fanlu Technology Co., Ltd., doing business as Smfanlus, based in China.

hazards: The recalled bicycle lights violate the mandatory standard for consumer products with button cell and coin batteries because they contain button cell batteries that can be accessed easily by children, posing an ingestion hazard. Additionally, the bicycle lights do not have the warnings required under Reese's Law. When button cell or coin batteries are swallowed, the ingested batteries can cause serious injuries, internal chemical burns, and death.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled bicycle lights immediately and contact Smfanlus for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to remove and properly dispose of the batteries and send a photo of the destroyed pieces of the light components to qumeneybicyclelightsrecall@outlook.com to receive a full refund. Note: Button cell batteries are hazardous. Batteries should be disposed of or recycled by following local hazardous waste procedures.
retailers: Online at Amazon.com from March 2024 through February 2026 for between $7 and $10 (6-pack) and between $15 and $18 (24-pack).

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product

Images of the recalled products show the battery compartment position marked with a red circle for reference.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets. To receive a full refund, contact Smfanlus by email at qumeneybicyclelightsrecall@outlook.com or online at their Amazon seller page and click "Recall" at the top.

Consumers will be instructed to remove and properly dispose of the batteries following local hazardous waste procedures. They must send a photo of the destroyed light components to the provided email address to complete the refund process.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled bicycle lights contain button cell batteries that can be easily accessed by children. If swallowed, these batteries can cause serious injuries such as internal chemical burns and even death. This ingestion hazard is especially dangerous because button cell batteries can cause severe damage quickly if ingested.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected

headline: Acer America Corporation Recalls AES015 Two-Wheeled Folding Electric Scooters Over Fall Risk
description: Acer recalls AES015 folding electric scooters due to a loose screw that can cause unexpected folding and fall injuries. About 510 units sold in the US.
keywords: Acer AES015 Two-Wheeled Folding Electric Scooters
A loose screw can cause the front tube to fold down unexpectedly, posing a risk of injury from a fall hazard.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Acer America Corporation is recalling about 510 AES015 two-wheeled folding electric scooters sold in the US because a loose screw can cause the front tube to fold down unexpectedly, creating a fall hazard. Consumers should stop using the scooter immediately and contact Acer for a free repair tool to tighten the screw.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves the Acer AES015 two-wheeled folding electric scooters designed for compact transport and storage by folding the front tube down to the platform. The scooters are black with green and white decals on the front tube and platform sides, and the wheels feature a green circle around the rim. The model number AES015 is located on a label on the front tube.

About 510 units were sold in the United States, with an additional 311 sold in Canada and 150 in Mexico. These scooters were sold online at Acer.com, Amazon.com, and in stores at BrandMart between June 2023 and February 2026, priced between $70 and $245.

The recall was announced on April 30, 2026, after reports indicated a loose screw could cause the front tube to fold down unexpectedly, posing a fall hazard. While no injuries have been reported in the U.S., there was one international report of a fall resulting in minor abrasions.

products.Name: Acer AES015 Two-Wheeled Folding Electric Scooters
products.NumberOfUnits: About 510 (In addition, about 311 were sold in Canada and about 150 were sold in Mexico)

hazards: A loose screw can cause the front tube to fold down unexpectedly, posing a risk of injury from a fall hazard.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled folding electric scooter immediately and contact Acer at https://www.acer.com/us-en/support/alerts-recalls for instructions on how to tighten the screw. Free repair tools will be available to consumers upon request.
injuries: None reported in the United States (and one report of the tube folding down resulting in a fall and minor abrasions internationally).
retailers: Online at Acer.com, Amazon.com and in stores at BrandMart from between June 2023 and February 2026 for between $245 and $70.
importers: Acer America Corporation, of Irvine, California

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify if your scooter is part of this recall, look for the following features:

  • Model number AES015 printed on a label located on the front tube of the scooter.
  • The scooter is black with green and white decals on the front tube and the sides of the platform.
  • The wheels have a distinctive green circle around the rim.

Refer to the label on the front tube to confirm the model number before taking any further steps.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you own an Acer AES015 folding electric scooter, stop using it immediately to avoid the risk of falling. Contact Acer America Corporation for instructions on how to tighten the loose screw, which is the cause of the hazard. Acer will provide free repair tools upon request to secure the screw and make the scooter safe to use again.

You can reach Acer toll-free at 866-514-2237 from 6 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email at acer.esseries@acer.com, or visit https://www.acer.com/us-en/support/alerts-recalls for more information and support.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The hazard involves a loose screw on the folding mechanism of the scooter’s front tube. If this screw becomes loose, the front tube can fold down unexpectedly while the scooter is in use. This sudden folding can cause the rider to lose balance and fall, increasing the risk of injury. Although no injuries have been reported in the U.S., the risk of falls and related injuries is significant enough to warrant a recall and repair.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Consumers who purchased the Acer AES015 two-wheeled folding electric scooter from June 2023 through February 2026 in the United States, Canada, or Mexico are affected. The scooters were sold online at Acer.com, Amazon.com, and in physical stores such as BrandMart. If you own this model, you should check your scooter immediately.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Acer America Corporation toll-free at 866-514-2237 from 6 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email at acer.esseries@acer.com or online at https://www.acer.com/us-en/support/alerts-recalls or go to www.acer.com and click on "Product Recalls" for more information.

faq.answer: Check the model number AES015 on the label located on the front tube. The scooter is black with green and white decals and has wheels with a green circle around the rim.

faq.answer: Stop using the scooter immediately and contact Acer for free repair tools and instructions to tighten the screw.

headline: DGIVOVO US Recalls Electric Pressure Washers Due to Risk of Shock and Electrocution
description: DGIVOVO US recalls electric pressure washers sold online due to shock and electrocution hazards. Consumers should stop use and request a free replacement.
keywords: The recalled pressure washers lack an integral ground-fault circuit-interrupter (GFCI), posing a serious risk of injury or death from shock and electrocution hazards.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: DGIVOVO US has recalled about 50 electric pressure washers sold online at Amazon.com between October 2025 and January 2026 due to serious shock and electrocution risks. Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled washers and contact DGIVOVO US for a free replacement after destroying the product as instructed.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves DGIVOVO US-branded electric pressure washers that are red, green, or blue with black accents. The units measure approximately seven inches high, nine inches wide, and eight inches deep. Included accessories are a 500 ml spray can attachment, foam cannon, four connect nozzles, a 20-foot hose, and two rollers. The model number "C4012PLUS-1500C" is printed on a label located on the back of the pressure washers.

Approximately 50 units were sold online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through January 2026 for about $90 each. The pressure washers were imported by Ningbopingyao E-Commerce Ltd., doing business as DGIVOVO US, based in China. No injuries have been reported related to this recall.

The recall was announced on April 30, 2026, due to the lack of an integral ground-fault circuit interrupter (GFCI), which poses a serious risk of shock and electrocution to users.

hazards: The recalled pressure washers lack an integral ground-fault circuit-interrupter (GFCI), posing a serious risk of injury or death from shock and electrocution hazards.
remedies: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled pressure washers and contact DGIVOVO US for a free replacement pressure washer of at least equal value to the recalled pressure washer, including shipping. Consumers will be asked to destroy the pressure washer by unplugging it and cutting the power cord in half and send a photo of the destroyed power washer to dgivovo@163.com. headline: Recall of Earthtec 7 3/8-Inch Pool Drain Covers Due to Safety Violations and Risk of Serious Injury or Death
description: Earthtec pool drain covers are recalled for entrapment and drowning hazards violating the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool & Spa Safety Act. About 211 units sold online.
keywords: The recalled drain covers violate the entrapment protection standards of the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act (VGBA), posing entrapment and drowning hazards to swimmers and bathers.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Earthtec 7 3/8-inch pool drain covers sold online are recalled because they do not meet federal safety standards, posing serious entrapment and drowning risks. Consumers should stop using these covers immediately and contact Tonyidea for a refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Earthtec pool drain covers measuring 7 3/8 inches in diameter, made of white ABS plastic, and secured with two screws. Approximately 211 units were sold online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through April 2026 for about $15 each.

The drain covers violate the entrapment protection standards set by the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act (VGBA). This noncompliance creates a risk of entrapment and drowning hazards for swimmers and bathers using pools equipped with these covers.

The manufacturer is Shenzhen Qiangonghui Technology Co., Ltd., doing business as Tonyidea, based in China. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

hazards: The recalled drain covers violate the entrapment protection standards of the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act (VGBA), posing entrapment and drowning hazards to swimmers and bathers.
remedies: Pool owners, pool operators and consumers should stop using pools with the recalled pool drain covers immediately. To receive a refund, consumers should remove the drain cover from the pool, and destroy it, take a photo of the removed cover, and send it to Tonyidea by email at earthtecrecall@outlook.com. Ensure all pools and spas have VGBA-compliant drain covers and teach children to stay away from drains.
retailers: Online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through April 2026 for about $15.
manufacturers: Shenzhen Qiangonghui Technology Co., Ltd., dba Tonyidea

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should check their pool drain covers for the following identifying features:

  • Brand: Earthtec
  • Size: 7 3/8 inches in diameter
  • Color: White
  • Material: ABS plastic
  • Includes two screws for installation

Photos of the recalled product show the front, back, and top views of the drain cover for comparison. If your pool drain cover matches these details, it is part of the recall.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Pool owners and operators should immediately stop using pools with the recalled Earthtec drain covers. To obtain a refund, consumers must:

  • Remove the drain cover from the pool
  • Destroy the removed drain cover
  • Take a photo of the removed cover
  • Email the photo to Tonyidea at earthtecrecall@outlook.com to request a refund

Additionally, ensure that all pools and spas have drain covers compliant with the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act. It is also important to teach children to stay away from pool drains to prevent accidents.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled Earthtec pool drain covers do not comply with federal safety standards designed to prevent swimmers from becoming trapped by suction at pool drains. Entrapment can lead to serious injury or drowning if a swimmer’s body, hair, or limbs are caught by the suction force. The Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act requires drain covers to meet specific safety criteria to reduce this risk, which these covers fail to meet.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects anyone who owns or operates a swimming pool equipped with the Earthtec 7 3/8-inch pool drain covers purchased online from Amazon.com between October 2025 and April 2026. Pool owners, operators, and consumers should verify if their drain covers are part of this recall to ensure safety.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Tonyidea by email at earthtecrecall@outlook.com.

faq.question: What hazard do the Earthtec pool drain covers pose?
faq.answer: The drain covers violate federal safety standards, creating entrapment and drowning hazards by failing to prevent swimmers from being trapped by suction at pool drains.

faq.question: Where were the recalled Earthtec pool drain covers sold?
faq.answer: They were sold online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through April 2026 for about $15 each.

faq.question: How can I get a refund for the recalled drain covers?
faq.answer: Remove and destroy the drain cover, take a photo of it, and email the photo to Tonyidea at earthtecrecall@outlook.com to request a refund.

headline: Recall of StrikeLock Hoistway Door Locking Device for Residential Elevators Poses Serious Fall and Injury Risks
description: Residential Elevators recalls about 450 StrikeLock hoistway door locking devices due to risk of elevator movement with door open, causing fall and injury hazards.
keywords: Residential Elevators StrikeLock Hoistway Door Locking Device
The elevator's StrikeLock hoistway door locking device can allow the elevator cab to move with a landing door open, posing a risk of serious injury or death due to fall and injury hazards.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The StrikeLock hoistway door locking device installed on some residential elevators can allow the elevator cab to move while the landing door is open, creating a serious risk of falls and injuries. About 450 units sold from April 2024 to March 2026 are affected. Consumers should stop using elevators with this device and contact Residential Elevators or their local dealer for a free repair.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device, a silver-colored component installed in the landing door jamb of residential elevator hoistway doors. It resembles a standard door latch plate and is designed to secure the landing door.

About 450 of these devices were sold by Residential Elevators and authorized dealers nationwide from April 2024 through March 2026, typically installed as part of residential elevator systems costing around $1,000. The device was manufactured and distributed by Residential Elevators, Inc., based in Crawfordville, Florida.

The hazard arises because the StrikeLock device can allow the elevator cab to move even when the landing door is open, posing a risk of serious injury or death from falls and other injuries. No injuries have been reported to date.

products.Name: Residential Elevators StrikeLock Hoistway Door Locking Device

hazards: The elevator's StrikeLock hoistway door locking device can allow the elevator cab to move with a landing door open, posing a risk of serious injury or death due to fall and injury hazards.
remedies: Consumers should immediately stop using the residential elevator systems where StrikeLock hoistway door locking devices are installed and contact Residential Elevators or the local elevator dealer that installed their residential elevator system to schedule a free repair of their StrikeLock hoistway door locking devices. Consumers should contact Residential Elevators or the local elevator dealer that installed their residential elevator system if they are uncertain whether their residential elevator has a StrikeLock hoistway door locking device installed.
retailers: Residential Elevators and authorized elevator dealers nationwide from April 2024 through March 2026 for about $1,000.
distributors: Residential Elevators, Inc., of Crawfordville, Florida

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify if your residential elevator has the recalled StrikeLock hoistway door locking device, look for a silver-colored door locking device installed in the landing door jamb of the elevator hoistway landing door. It resembles a standard door latch plate.

Images provided by the CPSC show the device both as a standalone component and installed on the elevator door. If you are unsure, contact Residential Elevators or your local elevator dealer for assistance in identifying the device.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If your residential elevator has the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device, immediately stop using the elevator to prevent risk of injury or death.

Contact Residential Elevators toll-free at 800-832-2004, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or email StrikeLock@Residentialelevators.com. You can also visit www.residentialelevators.com/recall-information/strikelock-locking-device or www.residentialelevators.com and click on "Recall Information" at the bottom of the page for more details.

Residential Elevators or your local elevator dealer will schedule a free repair to fix the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device. If you are uncertain whether your elevator has this device installed, contact them for verification.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
The StrikeLock hoistway door locking device is intended to keep the elevator landing door securely locked when the elevator cab is not present. However, this device can fail to prevent the elevator cab from moving while the landing door remains open. This creates a dangerous situation where someone could step into the elevator shaft or fall down the hoistway, leading to serious injury or death.

Because the elevator cab might move unexpectedly with the door open, the risk includes falls from height and crushing injuries. Users should consider this a significant safety hazard and take immediate action if their elevator system includes this device.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text:
This recall affects homeowners and residents with residential elevators equipped with the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device. The device was installed by Residential Elevators or authorized elevator dealers nationwide between April 2024 and March 2026.

If you have a residential elevator installed during this period, especially if purchased from Residential Elevators or their dealers, you should verify whether your system includes the StrikeLock device.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Residential Elevators toll-free at 800-832-2004 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email at StrikeLock@Residentialelevators.com, or online at www.residentialelevators.com/recall-information/strikelock-locking-device or www.residentialelevators.com and click on "Recall Information" at the bottom of the page for more information.

faq.answer: About 450 StrikeLock hoistway door locking devices installed in residential elevators are affected, sold from April 2024 through March 2026.

faq.answer: Stop using the elevator immediately and contact Residential Elevators or your local elevator dealer to schedule a free repair of the StrikeLock device.

The hazard arises because the StrikeLock device can allow the elevator cab to move even when the landing door is open, posing a risk of serious injury or death from falls and other injuries. No injuries have been reported to date.
editorialBrief.riskOverviewHtml:
The StrikeLock hoistway door locking device is intended to keep the elevator landing door securely locked when the elevator cab is not present. However, this device can fail to prevent the elevator cab from moving while the landing door remains open. This creates a dangerous situation where someone could step into the elevator shaft or fall down the hoistway, leading to serious injury or death.

Because the elevator cab might move unexpectedly with the door open, the risk includes falls from height and crushing injuries. Users should consider this a significant safety hazard and take immediate action if their elevator system includes this device.
editorialBrief.whoIsAffectedHtml:
This recall affects homeowners and residents with residential elevators equipped with the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device. The device was installed by Residential Elevators or authorized elevator dealers nationwide between April 2024 and March 2026.

If you have a residential elevator installed during this period, especially if purchased from Residential Elevators or their dealers, you should verify whether your system includes the StrikeLock device.
editorialBrief.howToIdentifyProductHtml:
To identify if your residential elevator has the recalled StrikeLock hoistway door locking device, look for a silver-colored door locking device installed in the landing door jamb of the elevator hoistway landing door. It resembles a standard door latch plate.

Images provided by the CPSC show the device both as a standalone component and installed on the elevator door. If you are unsure, contact Residential Elevators or your local elevator dealer for assistance in identifying the device.
editorialBrief.whatToDoHtml:
If your residential elevator has the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device, immediately stop using the elevator to prevent risk of injury or death.

Contact Residential Elevators toll-free at 800-832-2004, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or email StrikeLock@Residentialelevators.com. You can also visit www.residentialelevators.com/recall-information/strikelock-locking-device or www.residentialelevators.com and click on "Recall Information" at the bottom of the page for more details.

Residential Elevators or your local elevator dealer will schedule a free repair to fix the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device. If you are uncertain whether your elevator has this device installed, contact them for verification.

headline: Hepo Adult Portable Bed Rails Recalled for Serious Injury and Death Risks from Entrapment and Asphyxiation
description: Hepo Adult Portable Bed Rails, model 110039, recalled for entrapment and asphyxiation hazards; about 2,200 units sold online from June 2025 to January 2026.
keywords: The recalled bed rails violate the mandatory standard for adult portable bed rails because users can become entrapped within the bed rail or between the bed rail and the side of the mattress, posing a serious entrapment hazard and risk of death by asphyxiation. The bed rails also do not meet structural stability or retention strap requirements, posing a fall hazard. The bed rails' push pins and push pin holes are incorrectly sized, posing a laceration hazard. In addition, the bed rails do not bear the required hazard warning labels.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The Hepo-branded adult portable bed rails, model 110039, have been recalled due to serious risks of entrapment, asphyxiation, falls, and lacerations. Consumers should stop using these bed rails immediately and contact Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online for a full refund and instructions on safe disposal.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Hepo Adult Portable Bed Rails, model 110039, imported by Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online and manufactured by Mobility Source Medical Technology Co., Ltd. of China. The bed rails feature white tubing with black foam rubber handle grips, a black fabric pouch, and measure approximately 16 inches wide by 25 inches high. The model number is printed on the original product packaging.

About 2,200 units were sold online at Walmart.com from November 2025 through January 2026 for about $100, and on Amazon.com from June 2025 through January 2026 for between $35 and $40. No injuries have been reported to date.

The recall was announced on April 30, 2026, due to violations of the mandatory standard for adult portable bed rails, posing serious safety hazards.

hazards: The recalled bed rails violate the mandatory standard for adult portable bed rails because users can become entrapped within the bed rail or between the bed rail and the side of the mattress, posing a serious entrapment hazard and risk of death by asphyxiation. The bed rails also do not meet structural stability or retention strap requirements, posing a fall hazard. The bed rails' push pins and push pin holes are incorrectly sized, posing a laceration hazard. In addition, the bed rails do not bear the required hazard warning labels.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled adult portable bed rails immediately and contact Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to destroy the bed rails by dismantling the bed rails, cutting off the storage bag and writing "RECALLED" on the upper and lower rails with permanent marker, take a photo of the destroyed rails and upload the photo to www.hepolife.com/bed-rail-safety-recall or email the photo to manager@lqxmedical.com.
retailers: Online at Walmart.com from November 2025 through January 2026 for about $100 Amazon.com from June 2025 through January 2026 for between $35 and $40.
importers: Lingqingxiangjiankangkejigufenyouxiangongsi (Lingqingxiang Health Technology Co., Ltd.) dba Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online Ltd., of China
manufacturers: Mobility Source Medical Technology Co., Ltd. of China

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should check for the following identifying features to determine if their bed rails are part of the recall:

  • Brand: Hepo
  • Model number: 110039 (found on the original product packaging)
  • Appearance: White tubing with black foam rubber handle grips and a black fabric pouch
  • Dimensions: Approximately 16 inches wide by 25 inches high

A photo of the recalled product is available from the CPSC recall notice for comparison.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Hepo Adult Portable Bed Rails. To receive a full refund, contact Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online by email at manager@lqxmedical.com or visit https://www.hepolife.com/bed-rail-safety-recall. On the website, click "Recall" at the top of the page for more information.

Consumers will be instructed to dismantle the bed rails, cut off the storage bag, and mark "RECALLED" on the upper and lower rails with a permanent marker. They should then take a photo of the destroyed rails and upload it to the recall website or email it to manager@lqxmedical.com to complete the refund process.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled bed rails pose multiple hazards. Users can become entrapped within the bed rail or between the bed rail and the mattress, which can lead to serious injury or death by asphyxiation. The product also fails to meet structural stability and retention strap requirements, increasing the risk of falls. Additionally, incorrectly sized push pins and holes create a laceration hazard. The bed rails lack the required hazard warning labels, which could prevent users from recognizing these risks.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects consumers who purchased Hepo Adult Portable Bed Rails, model 110039, online from Walmart.com or Amazon.com between June 2025 and January 2026. These bed rails are intended for adult use and are typically used to provide support and prevent falls from beds.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online by email at manager@lqxmedical.com or online at https://www.hepolife.com/bed-rail-safety-recall or https://www.hepolife.com and click "Recall" at the top of the page for more information.

faq.question: What specific hazards do the recalled Hepo Adult Portable Bed Rails present?
faq.answer: The bed rails pose serious risks of entrapment and asphyxiation, structural instability leading to falls, and lacerations from incorrectly sized push pins and holes. They also lack required hazard warning labels.

faq.question: Where and when were the recalled bed rails sold?
faq.answer: About 2,200 units were sold online at Walmart.com from November 2025 through January 2026 and on Amazon.com from June 2025 through January 2026.

faq.question: How can I identify if my bed rails are part of this recall?
faq.answer: Check for the Hepo brand, model number 110039 on the original packaging, white tubing with black foam rubber handle grips, a black fabric pouch, and dimensions of about 16 inches wide by 25 inches high.

headline: UHOMEPRO Recalls 5-Drawer Dressers Due to Serious Injury and Death Risks from Tip-Over
description: UHOMEPRO recalls 5-drawer dressers sold online at Walmart.com due to tip-over hazards risking serious injury or death to children. About 5,190 units affected.
keywords: The recalled dressers are unstable if they are not anchored to the wall, posing tip-over and entrapment hazards that can result in risks of serious injuries or death to children. The dressers violate the mandatory safety standard as required by the STURDY Act.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: UHOMEPRO is recalling about 5,190 black and white 5-drawer dressers sold online at Walmart.com between November 2025 and January 2026 because they can tip over if not anchored, posing serious injury or death risks to children. Consumers should stop using unanchored dressers, keep children away, and contact UHOMEPRO for a full refund and disposal instructions.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves UHOMEPRO 5-Drawer Dressers made of particle board, available in black and white finishes. Each dresser measures 26 inches long by 15.7 inches wide by 38.6 inches tall and weighs about 66 pounds. The dresser features five drawers, each with a golden handle.

About 5,190 units were sold online at Walmart.com from November 2025 through January 2026 for approximately $100. The dressers were imported by UHOMEPRO Inc., based in China.

The dressers violate the mandatory safety standard required by the STURDY Act due to instability if not anchored to a wall. No injuries have been reported to date.

hazards: The recalled dressers are unstable if they are not anchored to the wall, posing tip-over and entrapment hazards that can result in risks of serious injuries or death to children. The dressers violate the mandatory safety standard as required by the STURDY Act.
remedies: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled dressers if they are not anchored to the wall and place them in an area that children cannot access. Contact UHOMEPRO for instructions on how to identify affected units and dispose of the dressers to receive a full refund. Consumers will be asked to write the word "recalled" on the top and all sides of the dresser with a permanent marker and to submit a photo to uhomeprorecall@outlook.com demonstrating disposal of the product.
retailers: Online at Walmart.com from November 2025 through January 2026 for about $100.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify the recalled dresser, look for the following characteristics:

  • Brand: UHOMEPRO
  • Type: 5-drawer dresser made of particle board
  • Colors: Black or white finish
  • Dimensions: Approximately 26 inches long, 15.7 inches wide, and 38.6 inches tall
  • Weight: About 66 pounds
  • Each drawer has a golden handle

Images of the dresser’s front, side, and back views are available from the CPSC recall notice for visual confirmation.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Consumers should immediately stop using the dresser if it is not anchored to a wall and move it to a location inaccessible to children. Contact UHOMEPRO by email at uhomeprorecall@outlook.com for instructions on identifying affected units and how to dispose of the dresser safely.

As part of the remedy, consumers will be asked to write the word "recalled" on the top and all sides of the dresser with a permanent marker and submit a photo showing the dresser marked and disposed of to UHOMEPRO via email. A full refund will be provided.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled dressers are unstable when not anchored to a wall, creating a tip-over hazard. This instability can cause the dresser to fall forward, posing serious risks of injury or death, especially to children who may climb on or pull the drawers. Additionally, the tip-over can lead to entrapment hazards where children could become trapped underneath or behind the dresser. The dresser does not meet the mandatory safety requirements designed to prevent such tip-over incidents.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Consumers who purchased the UHOMEPRO 5-Drawer Dressers online at Walmart.com between November 2025 and January 2026 are affected by this recall. The dresser is commonly used in homes with children, who are at the highest risk of injury from tip-over and entrapment hazards.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: UHOMEPRO by email at uhomeprorecall@outlook.com.

faq.question: Why are these UHOMEPRO dressers being recalled?
faq.answer: They are being recalled because they can tip over if not anchored to a wall, posing serious injury or death risks from tip-over and entrapment hazards, especially to children.

faq.question: How can I tell if my dresser is part of the recall?
faq.answer: Check if your dresser is a UHOMEPRO 5-drawer model made of particle board, in black or white, with golden handles, measuring about 26 x 15.7 x 38.6 inches, and if it was purchased online at Walmart.com between November 2025 and January 2026.

faq.question: What should I do if I have a recalled dresser?
faq.answer: Stop using it immediately if it is not anchored, keep it away from children, and contact UHOMEPRO at uhomeprorecall@outlook.com for instructions on how to identify, mark, dispose of the dresser, and receive a full refund.

headline: Malker Bicycle Light Sets Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Button Cell Battery Ingestion
description: Malker Bicycle Light Sets sold on Amazon are recalled for accessible button cell batteries posing ingestion hazards to children. Consumers should stop use and seek.
keywords: The recalled bicycle lights violate the mandatory standard for consumer products with button cell and coin batteries because they contain button cell batteries that can be accessed easily by children, posing an ingestion hazard. Additionally, the bicycle lights do not have the warnings required under Reese's Law. When button cell or coin batteries are swallowed, the ingested batteries can cause serious injuries, internal chemical burns and death.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Malker Bicycle Light Sets sold on Amazon between October and November 2025 are recalled because their button cell batteries can be easily accessed by children, posing a serious ingestion hazard. Consumers should stop using these lights immediately and contact MalkerDirect for a full refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Malker Bicycle Light Sets sold in 4- or 8-pack sizes. The lights come in red, black, or white silicone covers, emitting red or white light depending on the color. Each light contains two preinstalled CR2032 button cell batteries. The packaging is labeled with "Malker Bicycle Light" on the front.

About 7,000 units were sold online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through November 2025, priced between $7 and $10. The products were distributed by Shenzhen Maikeer Industrial Co., Ltd., doing business as MalkerDirect, based in China.

No injuries have been reported to date related to this recall.

hazards: The recalled bicycle lights violate the mandatory standard for consumer products with button cell and coin batteries because they contain button cell batteries that can be accessed easily by children, posing an ingestion hazard. Additionally, the bicycle lights do not have the warnings required under Reese's Law. When button cell or coin batteries are swallowed, the ingested batteries can cause serious injuries, internal chemical burns and death.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled bicycle lights immediately and contact MalkerDirect for a full refund. Consumers should throw the bicycle lights away and send a photo of the product in the trash to USMalkerCustomerservice@outlook.com. Note: Button cell batteries are hazardous. Batteries should be disposed of or recycled by following local hazardous waste procedures.
retailers: Online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through November 2025 for between $7 and $10.
distributors: Shenzhen Maikeer Industrial Co., Ltd., doing business as MalkerDirect, of China

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should check for bicycle light sets labeled "Malker Bicycle Light" on the front packaging. The recalled sets come in 4- or 8-pack sizes with the following configurations:

  • 8 Piece: 4 red and 4 black lights
  • 4 Piece: 2 red and 2 black lights
  • 4 Piece: 2 red and 2 white lights
  • 4 Piece: 4 black lights

Each light contains two preinstalled CR2032 button cell batteries. The silicone covers are red, black, or white, and the lights emit red or white light accordingly.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Consumers should stop using the recalled Malker Bicycle Light Sets immediately. They should dispose of the lights by following local hazardous waste procedures for button cell batteries. To receive a full refund, consumers must contact MalkerDirect by emailing a photo of the product in the trash to USMalkerCustomerservice@outlook.com.

Do not attempt to remove the batteries yourself, as button cell batteries are hazardous. Proper disposal is important to prevent injury or environmental harm.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled bicycle lights contain button cell batteries that can be easily accessed by children. If swallowed, these batteries can cause serious internal chemical burns, injuries, or even death. The products do not meet mandatory safety standards requiring secure battery compartments and warning labels. This ingestion hazard is especially dangerous because children may not show immediate symptoms but can suffer severe damage quickly.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects anyone who purchased Malker Bicycle Light Sets sold on Amazon between October and November 2025. The products were sold in 4- or 8-pack sets with red, black, or white silicone covers. Households with young children are particularly at risk due to the battery ingestion hazard.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: MalkerDirect by email at USMalkerCustomerservice@outlook.com.

faq.answer: They were sold online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through November 2025.

faq.answer: Stop using the lights immediately, dispose of them safely, and email a photo of the product in the trash to USMalkerCustomerservice@outlook.com to receive a full refund from MalkerDirect.
